207	| `validate-desc` | done (0.48.0-4) — `validate_desc(&Context, &ValidateDescParams)`; `From<&ValidateDescArgs>` |
208	| `fix-desc` | done (0.48.0-5) — `fix_desc(&Context, &FixDescParams)`; `From<&FixDescArgs>` |
209	| `push` | done (0.48.0-6) — `push(&Context, &PushParams)`; `From<&PushArgs>` (collapses the two bookmark spellings) |

file.content:
## Migration A — args → Context + Params

Port each subcommand's `pub fn x(args: &XxxArgs)` to
`pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams)`, adding an
`XxxParams` flat struct + a `From` (or `TryFrom`, if the
conversion is fallible) at the binary edge. `init` (0.44.0) is
the worked example; `finalize` (0.46.0) added the `TryFrom` /
`Context.log` variant; the `0.48.0` cycle swept the rest of the
standalone subcommands. Only `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`
remain — their Migration A is bundled into the future
"CommonArgs sweep" (Migration A + B are entangled for the
`CommonArgs`-flattening subcommands). The live checklist is the
"Subcommand layer / CLI decoupling" item in
[`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md).

| Subcommand | Status |
| --- | --- |
| `init` | done (0.44.0) — `init(&Context, &InitParams)`; `From<&InitArgs>` |
| `finalize` | done (0.46.0) — `finalize(&Context, &FinalizeParams)`; `TryFrom<&FinalizeArgs>` (fallible boundary); `--log` moved onto `Context` |
| `symlink` | done (0.48.0-1) — `symlink(&Context, &SymlinkParams)`; `From<&SymlinkArgs>` |
| `clone` | done (0.48.0-2) — `clone_repo(&Context, &CloneParams)`; `From<&CloneArgs>` |
| `sync` | done (0.48.0-3) — `sync(&Context, &SyncParams)`; `From<&SyncArgs>` |
| `validate-desc` | done (0.48.0-4) — `validate_desc(&Context, &ValidateDescParams)`; `From<&ValidateDescArgs>` |
| `fix-desc` | done (0.48.0-5) — `fix_desc(&Context, &FixDescParams)`; `From<&FixDescArgs>` |
| `push` | done (0.48.0-6) — `push(&Context, &PushParams)`; `From<&PushArgs>` (collapses the two bookmark spellings) |
| `chid` | done (0.49.0-1) — `chid(&Context, &ChidParams)`; `From<&ChidArgs>`. Migration B for `CommonArgs` (→ `options_flags/` leaf) still pending — the "CommonArgs sweep" todo. |
| `desc` | Migration A pending (0.49.0-2) — rides the chid/desc/list/show port; Migration B still on the CommonArgs sweep |
| `list` | deferred — rides the CommonArgs sweep (Migration A + B entangled) |
| `show` | deferred — rides the CommonArgs sweep (Migration A + B entangled) |

Out of scope for these ports (deferred until a real consumer
surfaces): typed errors, returned outcomes vs `println!`, the
`ProgressSink`, and `Context` fields beyond `UserConfig` + the
`--log` path (`finalize` surfaced `log`).

conversion is fallible) at the binary edge. `init` (0.44.0) is
the worked example; `finalize` (0.46.0) added the `TryFrom` /
`Context.log` variant; the `0.48.0` cycle swept the standalone
subcommands; the `0.49.0` cycle finishes the four
`CommonArgs`-flattening ones (`chid` -1, `desc` -2, `list` -3,
`show` -4) — these embed a shared clap-free `CommonParams`. Their
Migration B (folding `CommonArgs` into the `options_flags/` leaf
model) stays the separate "CommonArgs sweep" todo. The live
checklist is the "Subcommand layer / CLI decoupling" item in
[`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md).

| Subcommand | Status |
| --- | --- |
| `init` | done (0.44.0) — `init(&Context, &InitParams)`; `From<&InitArgs>` |
| `finalize` | done (0.46.0) — `finalize(&Context, &FinalizeParams)`; `TryFrom<&FinalizeArgs>` (fallible boundary); `--log` moved onto `Context` |
| `symlink` | done (0.48.0-1) — `symlink(&Context, &SymlinkParams)`; `From<&SymlinkArgs>` |
| `clone` | done (0.48.0-2) — `clone_repo(&Context, &CloneParams)`; `From<&CloneArgs>` |
| `sync` | done (0.48.0-3) — `sync(&Context, &SyncParams)`; `From<&SyncArgs>` |
| `validate-desc` | done (0.48.0-4) — `validate_desc(&Context, &ValidateDescParams)`; `From<&ValidateDescArgs>` |
| `fix-desc` | done (0.48.0-5) — `fix_desc(&Context, &FixDescParams)`; `From<&FixDescArgs>` |
| `push` | done (0.48.0-6) — `push(&Context, &PushParams)`; `From<&PushArgs>` (collapses the two bookmark spellings) |
| `chid` | done (0.49.0-1) — `chid(&Context, &ChidParams)`; `From<&ChidArgs>` (wraps `CommonParams`) |
| `desc` | Migration A pending (0.49.0-2) — chid/desc/list/show port; Migration B on the CommonArgs sweep |
| `list` | Migration A pending (0.49.0-3) — chid/desc/list/show port; Migration B on the CommonArgs sweep |
| `show` | Migration A pending (0.49.0-4) — chid/desc/list/show port (`TryFrom`, `FileLimit` parse); Migration B on the CommonArgs sweep |

input.command: jj diff --stat -R .

content.text:
`0.49.0-1` (chid + `CommonParams`) is staged. Changes:

- **`src/common.rs`** — new `CommonParams { spec: DotSpec, header: Header, repos: Vec<PathBuf> }` (`#[derive(Debug, Clone)]`) + `impl From<&CommonArgs>` that runs `resolve_spec` (default rev `@`) and `resolve_header` at the binary edge. This is the shared piece `desc`/`list`/`show` will reuse.
- **`src/chid.rs`** — full rewrite: added the missing `//!` module docstring; added `ChidParams { common: CommonParams }` + `impl From<&ChidArgs>`; ported `chid(args)` → `chid(_ctx: &Context, params: &ChidParams)` (body reads `params.common.*`, `ctx` unused — uniform-signature placeholder, doc'd like `validate-desc`/`fix-desc`). All existing `ChidArgs` parse tests kept; added one `params_from_args` test exercising the new boundary conversion (`@.. 5` → 4 ancestors; `-L` → `Header::None`).
- **`src/main.rs`** — `Chid` dispatch arm now builds `Context::load(cli.log)` + `ChidParams::from(&chid_args)`, same shape as the other migrated arms. (`suppress_banner` still reads `a.common.no_label` off the args — unchanged.)
- **`ARCHITECTURE.md`** — `chid` row in both tables → migrated (0.49.0-1); reworked the Migration A intro + `desc`/`list`/`show` rows to reflect that the four `CommonArgs` subcommands are now this `0.49.0` cycle (not the future "CommonArgs sweep"), with Migration B for them still on the sweep.
- **`notes/todo.md`** — In Progress ladder: `0.49.0-0` flipped `(current)` → `(done)`, `0.49.0-1` → `(current)`. (Note: the `0.49.0-0` commit itself shipped with `0.49.0-0 (current)` — I should've flipped it pre-push; fixed forward here rather than amending the docs commit. Say the word if you'd rather I amend `@-`'s predecessor instead.)

Checks: `cargo fmt` (reformatted the `collect_ids` call in `chid.rs` to multi-line) / `clippy --all-targets -D warnings` clean / `cargo test` → 361+5+4+5 all pass / `cargo install --path . --locked` / smoke: `vc-x1 chid -L` and `vc-x1 chid @.. 3` produce correct output.

Please review. On approval I'll prep the commit title/body for a second approval before pushing.

content.content:
1	# options_flags — Reusable CLI Options and Flags
2	
3	Per-flag CLI surfaces shared across multiple subcommands. Each
4	shared option or flag (OF) lives in its own submodule so its
5	type, parser, and tests stay together.
6	
7	This module is the CLI-layer leaf store for "Migration B" in
8	[`../../ARCHITECTURE.md`](../../ARCHITECTURE.md); see there for
9	how the leaves fit the wider clap-args / `Context`+`Params`
10	split.
11	
12	## Architecture
13	
14	Three composition patterns:
15	
16	- **Leaf** — a `#[derive(Args)]` struct holding the flag(s),
17	  `value_parser`, default, and help text (via the field
18	  doc-comment). One `#[derive(Args)]` per logical OF — single
19	  flag or a small fixed pair (e.g. `push_retry`'s two fields).
20	  Help text aims to be generic enough for any reasonable
21	  consumer.
22	- **Bundle** — a `#[derive(Args)]` that flattens N leaves into a
23	  named role (e.g. `ProvisionOptionFlagBundle`). One
24	  `#[command(flatten)]` line at the consumer picks up the whole
25	  bundle.
26	- **Pattern A escape hatch** — when a consumer needs unique help
27	  text (or different defaults), it skips the leaf's flatten and
28	  inlines its own `#[arg(value_parser = …)]` field, reusing the
29	  leaf's typed value and parser.
30	
31	## Flag vs Option — classify by domain, not wire syntax
32	
33	Every leaf falls into one of two categories, picked by the value
34	domain it carries:
35	
36	- **Flag** — boolean domain. Wire form may be presence/absence
37	  (`--dry-run`), explicit negation (`--no-dry-run`), or value
38	  (`--dry-run=true|false`). All three express a `bool`. Type
39	  name suffix: `*Flag`.
40	- **Option** — non-boolean domain (string, integer, enum, path,
41	  …). Wire form is always `--name=<value>` (or `--name <value>`).
42	  Type name suffix: `*Option` (or `*Options` for a multi-field
43	  leaf like `PushRetryOptions`).
44	
45	Wire form is presentation; the category is the underlying domain.
46	
47	## Adding a new leaf
48	
49	1. Create `options_flags/<name>.rs` with a
50	   `#[derive(Args, Debug, Clone, Default)]` struct.
51	2. Pick the suffix per domain: `*Flag` (boolean) or `*Option(s)`
52	   (non-boolean).
53	3. Name the field(s):
54	   - **Single-field leaf** — call the field `value` (it holds the
55	     parsed value-side of the option) and declare the flag
56	     explicitly with `#[arg(long = "<flag>", …)]`. The consumer
57	     then reads `args.<leaf>.value` rather than doubling the flag
58	     name (`args.squash.value`, not `args.squash.squash`).
59	   - **Multi-field leaf** — descriptive per-field names
60	     (`push_retries`, `push_retry_delay`); clap derives each
61	     flag from its field name as usual.
62	   - The pre-existing single-field leaves (`scope`, `repo`,
63	     `dry_run`, `private`, `account`, `config`, `use_template`)
64	     predate this and still derive the flag from the field name;
65	     migrating them to `value` is tracked in
66	     [`../../notes/todo.md`](../../notes/todo.md).
67	4. Add `pub mod <name>;` to `mod.rs`.
68	5. If the leaf has explicit parsing logic, declare a unit-struct
69	   implementor of `FlagParser` (boolean domain) or `OptionParser`
70	   (non-boolean domain). Bare `Option<String>` leaves and
71	   presence/absence boolean leaves need no parser impl — clap
72	   handles them.
73	6. Add tests for any non-trivial parser/resolver logic.
74	7. Leaves do **not** implement a Bundle marker — bundles do.
75	
76	## Consuming an OF
77	
78	Default — flatten the leaf into your subcommand's `Args`:
79	
80	```rust
81	#[derive(Args)]
82	pub struct MyArgs {
83	    #[command(flatten)]
84	    pub config: ConfigOption,
85	    // ...
86	}
87	```
88	
89	Pattern A — when generic help doesn't fit:
90	
91	```rust
92	#[derive(Args)]
93	pub struct MyArgs {
94	    /// My subcommand-specific help for --config.
95	    #[arg(long = "config", value_name = "none|PATH",
96	          verbatim_doc_comment)]
97	    pub config: Option<String>,
98	    // ...
99	}
100	```
101	
102	A Pattern A consumer reuses the leaf's types (e.g. `ConfigKind`)
103	and parsers (e.g. `parse_config_kind`) but owns its own clap
104	attributes.
105	
106	## Consumer function shape
107	
108	Helper functions called by a subcommand body should accept the
109	relevant leaf type by reference rather than unpacking individual
110	fields at the call site:
111	
112	```rust
113	// Multi-field leaf — pass the whole leaf
114	fn run_retry(cmd: &str, args: &[&str], cwd: &Path,
115	             retry: &PushRetryOptions) -> Result<…> { … }
116	
117	run_retry("git", &["push", …], cwd, &args.provision.push_retry)?;
118	```
119	
120	This wins on:
121	- Readability — `&args.provision.push_retry` reads as a single
122	  unit; the function body works with `retry.push_retries` (no
123	  leaf-name doubling because the parameter name is the consumer's
124	  choice).
125	- Argument count — every multi-field leaf collapses N args into
126	  one ref, so chained helpers don't accumulate
127	  `clippy::too_many_arguments` warnings.
128	- Future-proofing — extending a leaf with another field is a
129	  zero-touch change at every call site.
130	
131	For **single-field leaves** (e.g. `SquashOption`, `DryRunFlag`,
132	`PrivateFlag`), direct read at the consumer site
133	(`args.squash.value`, or `args.provision.dry_run.dry_run` for the
134	pre-`value` leaves) is fine — wrapping the inner value in a
135	`&LeafType` parameter doesn't earn the indirection.
136	
137	## Marker traits
138	
139	Three Bundle markers — one per content classification. Bundles
140	implement exactly one. Leaves do **not** implement these markers
141	(their category lives in the type-name suffix).
142	
143	- `FlagBundle: clap::Args` — pure-boolean bundle (every
144	  constituent leaf is a Flag). Rare in practice.
145	- `OptionBundle: clap::Args` — pure-non-boolean bundle (every
146	  constituent leaf is an Option).
147	- `OptionFlagBundle: clap::Args` — mixed bundle (constituents
148	  include both Flag and Option leaves). Most common in practice.
149	
150	Two Parser traits — conditional contracts implemented only when
151	a leaf has explicit parsing logic (custom `value_parser`):
152	
153	- `FlagParser` — boolean-domain parser. Implemented only for
154	  boolean leaves that take a value form (`--flag=true|false`).
155	  Presence/absence flags need no impl; clap parses directly.
156	- `OptionParser` — non-boolean-domain parser. Implemented only
157	  for leaves with custom parsing logic (e.g. `ScopeParser`,
158	  `RepoParser`); bare `Option<String>` leaves need no impl.
159	
160	`FlagParser` and `OptionParser` share an identical method shape;
161	the difference is documentation (which value-domain the parser
162	targets).
163	
164	All five traits are documentation-level — clap derive doesn't
165	see them; the discipline is that every leaf and bundle declares
166	the appropriate ones explicitly.
167	
168	## Bundle marker discipline
169	
170	Bundle markers aren't compiler-checked against contents. If a
171	bundle's leaf set shifts category — e.g. a pure-Flag bundle gains
172	an Option leaf — you must update the marker by hand
173	(`FlagBundle` → `OptionFlagBundle`). No lint, no failure; silent
174	drift if forgotten. Worth a glance at the marker line whenever a
175	bundle's `#[command(flatten)]` set changes.
176	
177	## Pattern A worked example
178	
179	`init`'s `--config` field originally carried init-specific help
180	("Only valid with `--scope=por`..."). It currently flattens
181	`ConfigOption` for the generic help; the `--scope=por` constraint
182	surfaces in `init`'s preflight error. If the help-text generality
183	becomes a usability problem, init will switch to Pattern A as the
184	worked example.
185	
186	## Layout note
187	
188	OFs currently sit as flat `<name>.rs` files alongside this
189	README. Bundle modules carry the `_bundle` suffix
190	(e.g. `provision_bundle.rs`) so the leaf/bundle split is visible
191	in the directory listing without opening files. If an individual
192	OF accumulates enough rationale, edge-case detail, or examples
193	to outgrow doc-comments, it will graduate to a `<name>/mod.rs` +
194	`<name>/README.md` subdirectory layout. Mechanical when needed;
195	not done preemptively.

file.content:
# options_flags — Reusable CLI Options and Flags

Per-flag CLI surfaces shared across multiple subcommands. Each
shared option or flag (OF) lives in its own submodule so its
type, parser, and tests stay together.

This module is the CLI-layer leaf store for "Migration B" in
[`../../ARCHITECTURE.md`](../../ARCHITECTURE.md); see there for
how the leaves fit the wider clap-args / `Context`+`Params`
split.

## Architecture

Three composition patterns:

- **Leaf** — a `#[derive(Args)]` struct holding the flag(s),
  `value_parser`, default, and help text (via the field
  doc-comment). One `#[derive(Args)]` per logical OF — single
  flag or a small fixed pair (e.g. `push_retry`'s two fields).
  Help text aims to be generic enough for any reasonable
  consumer.
- **Bundle** — a `#[derive(Args)]` that flattens N leaves into a
  named role (e.g. `ProvisionOptionFlagBundle`). One
  `#[command(flatten)]` line at the consumer picks up the whole
  bundle.
- **Pattern A escape hatch** — when a consumer needs unique help
  text (or different defaults), it skips the leaf's flatten and
  inlines its own `#[arg(value_parser = …)]` field, reusing the
  leaf's typed value and parser.

## Flag vs Option — classify by domain, not wire syntax

Every leaf falls into one of two categories, picked by the value
domain it carries:

- **Flag** — boolean domain. Wire form may be presence/absence
  (`--dry-run`), explicit negation (`--no-dry-run`), or value
  (`--dry-run=true|false`). All three express a `bool`. Type
  name suffix: `*Flag`.
- **Option** — non-boolean domain (string, integer, enum, path,
  …). Wire form is always `--name=<value>` (or `--name <value>`).
  Type name suffix: `*Option` (or `*Options` for a multi-field
  leaf like `PushRetryOptions`).

Wire form is presentation; the category is the underlying domain.

## Adding a new leaf

1. Create `options_flags/<name>.rs` with a
   `#[derive(Args, Debug, Clone, Default)]` struct.
2. Pick the suffix per domain: `*Flag` (boolean) or `*Option(s)`
   (non-boolean).
3. Name the field(s):
   - **Single-field leaf** — call the field `value` (it holds the
     parsed value-side of the option) and declare the flag
     explicitly with `#[arg(long = "<flag>", …)]`. The consumer
     then reads `args.<leaf>.value` rather than doubling the flag
     name (`args.squash.value`, not `args.squash.squash`).
   - **Multi-field leaf** — descriptive per-field names
     (`push_retries`, `push_retry_delay`); clap derives each
     flag from its field name as usual.
   - The pre-existing single-field leaves (`scope`, `repo`,
     `dry_run`, `private`, `account`, `config`, `use_template`)
     predate this and still derive the flag from the field name;
     migrating them to `value` is tracked in
     [`../../notes/todo.md`](../../notes/todo.md).
4. Add `pub mod <name>;` to `mod.rs`.
5. If the leaf has explicit parsing logic, declare a unit-struct
   implementor of `FlagParser` (boolean domain) or `OptionParser`
   (non-boolean domain). Bare `Option<String>` leaves and
   presence/absence boolean leaves need no parser impl — clap
   handles them.
6. Add tests for any non-trivial parser/resolver logic.
7. Leaves do **not** implement a Bundle marker — bundles do.

## Consuming an OF

Default — flatten the leaf into your subcommand's `Args`:

```rust
#[derive(Args)]
pub struct MyArgs {
    #[command(flatten)]
    pub config: ConfigOption,
    // ...
}
```

Pattern A — when generic help doesn't fit:

```rust
#[derive(Args)]
pub struct MyArgs {
    /// My subcommand-specific help for --config.
    #[arg(long = "config", value_name = "none|PATH",
          verbatim_doc_comment)]
    pub config: Option<String>,
    // ...
}
```

A Pattern A consumer reuses the leaf's types (e.g. `ConfigKind`)
and parsers (e.g. `parse_config_kind`) but owns its own clap
attributes.

## Consumer function shape

Helper functions called by a subcommand body should accept the
relevant leaf type by reference rather than unpacking individual
fields at the call site:

```rust
// Multi-field leaf — pass the whole leaf
fn run_retry(cmd: &str, args: &[&str], cwd: &Path,
             retry: &PushRetryOptions) -> Result<…> { … }

run_retry("git", &["push", …], cwd, &args.provision.push_retry)?;
```

This wins on:
- Readability — `&args.provision.push_retry` reads as a single
  unit; the function body works with `retry.push_retries` (no
  leaf-name doubling because the parameter name is the consumer's
  choice).
- Argument count — every multi-field leaf collapses N args into
  one ref, so chained helpers don't accumulate
  `clippy::too_many_arguments` warnings.
- Future-proofing — extending a leaf with another field is a
  zero-touch change at every call site.

For **single-field leaves** (e.g. `SquashOption`, `DryRunFlag`,
`PrivateFlag`), direct read at the consumer site
(`args.squash.value`, or `args.provision.dry_run.dry_run` for the
pre-`value` leaves) is fine — wrapping the inner value in a
`&LeafType` parameter doesn't earn the indirection.

## Marker traits

Three Bundle markers — one per content classification. Bundles
implement exactly one. Leaves do **not** implement these markers
(their category lives in the type-name suffix).

- `FlagBundle: clap::Args` — pure-boolean bundle (every
  constituent leaf is a Flag). Rare in practice.
- `OptionBundle: clap::Args` — pure-non-boolean bundle (every
  constituent leaf is an Option).
- `OptionFlagBundle: clap::Args` — mixed bundle (constituents
  include both Flag and Option leaves). Most common in practice.

Two Parser traits — conditional contracts implemented only when
a leaf has explicit parsing logic (custom `value_parser`):

- `FlagParser` — boolean-domain parser. Implemented only for
  boolean leaves that take a value form (`--flag=true|false`).
  Presence/absence flags need no impl; clap parses directly.
- `OptionParser` — non-boolean-domain parser. Implemented only
  for leaves with custom parsing logic (e.g. `ScopeParser`,
  `RepoParser`); bare `Option<String>` leaves need no impl.

`FlagParser` and `OptionParser` share an identical method shape;
the difference is documentation (which value-domain the parser
targets).

All five traits are documentation-level — clap derive doesn't
see them; the discipline is that every leaf and bundle declares
the appropriate ones explicitly.

## Bundle marker discipline

Bundle markers aren't compiler-checked against contents. If a
bundle's leaf set shifts category — e.g. a pure-Flag bundle gains
an Option leaf — you must update the marker by hand
(`FlagBundle` → `OptionFlagBundle`). No lint, no failure; silent
drift if forgotten. Worth a glance at the marker line whenever a
bundle's `#[command(flatten)]` set changes.

## Pattern A worked example

`init`'s `--config` field originally carried init-specific help
("Only valid with `--scope=por`..."). It currently flattens
`ConfigOption` for the generic help; the `--scope=por` constraint
surfaces in `init`'s preflight error. If the help-text generality
becomes a usability problem, init will switch to Pattern A as the
worked example.

## Layout note

OFs currently sit as flat `<name>.rs` files alongside this
README. Bundle modules carry the `_bundle` suffix
(e.g. `provision_bundle.rs`) so the leaf/bundle split is visible
in the directory listing without opening files. If an individual
OF accumulates enough rationale, edge-case detail, or examples
to outgrow doc-comments, it will graduate to a `<name>/mod.rs` +
`<name>/README.md` subdirectory layout. Mechanical when needed;
not done preemptively.

lastPrompt: hmm, CommonArgs is really a bundle and it should be in options_flags

content.text:
You're right that conceptually it belongs there — it's a shared, multi-subcommand arg struct, same role as `ProvisionOptionFlagBundle`. Two wrinkles on *how cleanly* it fits today:

1. **It's a fat single struct, not a leaf-bundle.** `options_flags/` is built around *leaves* (one flag or a small fixed pair) composed into *bundles* (`#[command(flatten)]` of N leaves). `CommonArgs` has 7 inline `#[arg]` fields (`pos_rev`, `pos_count`, `-r`, `-R`, `-n`, `-l`, `-L`). To put it there *properly* you'd decompose it into leaves first — which is exactly the "CommonArgs sweep" todo, and that one also swaps the `-R`/`--repo` leaf for the `--scope` path form. So "do it right" = a separate, bigger cycle (Migration B + a `--scope` rollout), not a drop-in.
2. **A file-move-as-is is possible** — `CommonArgs` lands in `src/options_flags/common_args.rs` (or `common_bundle.rs`) undecomposed, marked as a bundle "pending leaf decomposition." Low risk, but it parks a non-leaf/non-bundle thing in the leaf store that the sweep then has to take apart anyway.

`CommonParams` (the clap-free side I just added) stays in `common.rs` regardless — it bundles the resolved `DotSpec` / `Header` / repos that `common.rs`'s `for_each_repo` / `collect_ids` consume; that's its natural home.

content.content:
## Generalize --scope to all commands (design)

Surfaced 2026-04-23 (from the user, top of todo): vc-x1 commands
should support both single and dual repo modes. The technique
sketched for `vc-x1 push` (`--scope=app|claude|both`) is the
right shape, but the scope concept should be a project-wide
convention rather than push-only.

**Naming:** `--scope=app|other|both` — "other" rather than
"claude" because the second repo isn't always `.claude`; could
be a different bot or a different sibling project structure.

**Why scope-first** (before adding more commands or push
features): each new command that operates across both repos
would otherwise need its own ad-hoc handling. Locking in the
convention now means new commands inherit it for free, and
existing commands (sync, finalize, push) get retrofitted under
the same vocabulary.

**Rough scope of the change:**

- Shared CLI flag definition + parsing helper (likely in
  `common.rs`).
- `sync`: already accepts `-R` for single-repo. Migrate to
  `--scope`; alias or deprecate `-R` (TBD below).
- `push`: `--scope` per the existing design, with
  warn-on-WC-mismatch.
- `finalize`: already takes `--repo`; rationalize naming.
- New commands (`status` etc.) start with `--scope` from day 1.

**Open:** do we deprecate `-R` immediately, alias it, or keep
both indefinitely? Probably: alias for one minor cycle,
deprecate, remove. Decide before implementation.


============ chores-06 1305-1500 ============
### 0.41.0-3: capture --scope-everywhere direction

Notes-only step. Records the directional decisions that
came out of the 0.41.0-2 close-out conversation
(2026-04-26) so the rest of the cycle and the post-0.41.0
follow-ups don't relitigate them.

**Consistency principle (decided).** Every subcommand that
operates on one or both repos gets `--scope=code|bot|code,
bot`. No vocabulary forks across commands; `--scope` is the
project-wide convention. Existing per-command flags
(`-R/--repo`, `--repo`) stay as back-compat aliases where
they already exist; new commands inherit `--scope` from day
one.

**Per-command rollout map.**

- `init` — done (0.40.0). `--scope=code|bot|code,bot`.
- `sync` — done (0.41.0-2). `--scope` + `-R` (mutually
  exclusive); `-R` retained for arbitrary multi-repo.
- `push` — 0.41.0-4. State machine becomes scope-aware;
  single-side path skips `commit-claude` /
  `finalize-claude` and bookmarks one repo only.
- `finalize` — 0.41.0-5. `--scope` is canonical; existing
  `--repo` becomes a back-compat alias (overrides scope
  when present, prints a soft deprecation note, slated
  for removal in a future cycle). Does *not* downgrade to
  the "review-only / no-op" outcome the original 0.41.0-4
  sketch hedged toward.
- `clone` — post-0.41.0. Parallel to `init --scope`;
  single-repo clone for `vc-template-x1`-shape remotes.
- `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` — post-0.41.0. Same
  vocabulary as everywhere else, no rename to `--side`.
  Semantic: `--scope=code` validates code's commits
  against bot, `--scope=bot` reverses the direction,
  `--scope=code,bot` does both (the new default).
- `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show` — post-0.41.0. Add
  `--scope` via `CommonArgs` so all four inherit it
  in one cycle. Existing `-R` / `--repo` stays alongside.
- `symlink` — N/A. Bot-only by definition; scope doesn't
  apply.

**Deferred design questions** (caught in todo, not blocking
the rollout):

- `[workspace]` section name in `.vc-config.toml` reads as
  a Cargo workspace to Rust users but isn't one. Rename
  candidates: `[repo-list]` / `[project]` / `[dual-repo]`.
  Breaking change; needs migration.
- Help layout uniformity — clap auto-picks two-column vs
  over-under per command based on widest flag spec.
  `sync -h` is two-column, `init -h` is over-under. Force
  uniform via `next_line_help(true)` at the root.

Edits:

- `Cargo.toml`: bump to `0.41.0-3`.
- `CLAUDE.md`: `### Versioning` cross-references the new
  In Progress dev-ladder convention.
- `notes/chores-06.md`: refine the [71] cycle-step sketch
  to insert this `-3` notes step and renumber push (was
  `-3`) and finalize (was `-4`); add this subsection.
- `notes/todo.md`: three new Todo entries — `clone`
  `--scope`, `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` `--scope`,
  `CommonArgs` (`chid`/`desc`/`list`/`show`) `--scope`.
  In Progress reshaped — explanatory intro paragraph
  shows the dev-ladder format, and the active entry is
  rewritten as the bulleted ladder with
  `(done)` / `(current)` markers.

### 0.41.0-4: capture --scope enum vocabulary

Notes-only step. Mid-implementation rethink — the
original `-4` (push --scope on top of `Vec<Side>`)
exposed a vocabulary gap that the user surfaced
2026-04-26: `code` plays two distinct roles depending on
context (a *role* in a dual workspace; an implicit
*fallback to cwd* in POR). Conflating them muddies what
"obvious to the user" looks like. Splitting them honestly
is a type-model change, not a per-command implementation
detail — so the cycle pivots: capture the refined
vocabulary here, defer all command implementations to
0.42.0, and close 0.41.0 cleanly.

**Settled vocabulary.**

- `--scope` (short form `-s`) accepts:
  - `code` — the app side of a dual-repo workspace.
  - `bot` — the bot side of a dual-repo workspace.
  - `code,bot` / `bot,code` — both (commutative; same
    semantic, same default).
  - `<path>` — single-repo mode; ignore `.vc-config.toml`,
    operate on the given path. Path values must be
    prefixed (`./`, `/`, `~/`) to disambiguate from the
    keyword set. Tilde expansion mirrors init's existing
    behavior on `--repo-local` / `--repo-remote`.
- No discoverable `single` keyword. `--scope=.` is the
  cwd form; `--scope=/abs/path` or `--scope=./rel` is the
  explicit form. Bare names that aren't keywords error
  with a "did you mean `./foo`?" hint.

**Internal type model.**

```rust
pub enum Scope {
    Roles(Vec<Side>), // code | bot | code,bot
    Single(PathBuf),  // explicit path, single-repo mode
}
```

Replaces today's `Scope(pub Vec<Side>)`. The current
`has_code` / `has_bot` / `is_code_only` etc. helpers all
shift to operate on `Roles` only — `Single(_)` is a
distinct mode, not a one-element role list.

**Default-scope rules (revised).**

| Workspace state | `.vc-config.toml` shape | Default scope |
|---|---|---|
| Dual workspace | `path` + non-empty `other-repo` | `Roles([Code, Bot])` |
| Single-repo workspace | `path`, no `other-repo` | `Roles([Code])` |
| POR | (no file) | `Single(cwd)` |

The single-repo-workspace concept stays — it's a real,
distinct state with a non-trivial default (`Roles([Code])`
not `Single(...)`). `--scope=bot` errors there with
"no other-repo configured."

**Per-command applicability matrix.**

| Command | `Roles` accepted | `Single(_)` accepted |
|---|---|---|
| `init` | yes | no — init creates workspaces, not POR repos |
| `sync` | yes | yes |
| `push` | yes | yes |
| `finalize` | yes (currently has `--repo`; migrates to `--scope`) | yes |
| `clone` | yes | yes (single-repo clone target) |
| `validate-desc` | yes | **error** — validate compares two repos by definition; `Single` is meaningless |
| `fix-desc` | yes | **error** (same reason as validate-desc) |
| `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show` | yes | yes |
| `symlink` | n/a | n/a |

`Single` is *always* available as the explicit
"ignore-the-config" escape hatch on commands where it
applies, even inside a dual workspace — the user
explicitly bypasses the dispatcher. On commands where it
can't apply (validate-desc / fix-desc), errors with a
specific message rather than silently doing the wrong
thing.

**Flag-surface changes.**

- Drop `-R` / `--repo` everywhere. Currently sync has it
  (mutually exclusive with `--scope`); finalize has its
  own `--repo` (single repo). Both fold into the new
  `--scope=<path>` form.
- `--scope` gets short form `-s` for ergonomics.
- The path form covers what `-R` / `--repo` did before.
  Multi-repo (sync's old `-R . -R .claude` style) is
  deliberately not preserved — that capability had no
  real users beyond manually emulating today's default.

**Rationale for the cycle pivot.**

- The 0.41.0-3 capture committed to "consistency
  everywhere", which means push, finalize, and the
  remaining commands all need to agree on the type

- Closing 0.41.0 here produces a clean checkpoint: the
  scope foundation (0.40.0 init) and one consumer

toolUseResult.stdout:
## Generalize --scope to all commands (design)

Surfaced 2026-04-23 (from the user, top of todo): vc-x1 commands
should support both single and dual repo modes. The technique
sketched for `vc-x1 push` (`--scope=app|claude|both`) is the
right shape, but the scope concept should be a project-wide
convention rather than push-only.

**Naming:** `--scope=app|other|both` — "other" rather than
"claude" because the second repo isn't always `.claude`; could
be a different bot or a different sibling project structure.

**Why scope-first** (before adding more commands or push
features): each new command that operates across both repos
would otherwise need its own ad-hoc handling. Locking in the
convention now means new commands inherit it for free, and
existing commands (sync, finalize, push) get retrofitted under
the same vocabulary.

**Rough scope of the change:**

- Shared CLI flag definition + parsing helper (likely in
  `common.rs`).
- `sync`: already accepts `-R` for single-repo. Migrate to
  `--scope`; alias or deprecate `-R` (TBD below).
- `push`: `--scope` per the existing design, with
  warn-on-WC-mismatch.
- `finalize`: already takes `--repo`; rationalize naming.
- New commands (`status` etc.) start with `--scope` from day 1.

**Open:** do we deprecate `-R` immediately, alias it, or keep
both indefinitely? Probably: alias for one minor cycle,
deprecate, remove. Decide before implementation.


============ chores-06 1305-1500 ============
### 0.41.0-3: capture --scope-everywhere direction

Notes-only step. Records the directional decisions that
came out of the 0.41.0-2 close-out conversation
(2026-04-26) so the rest of the cycle and the post-0.41.0
follow-ups don't relitigate them.

**Consistency principle (decided).** Every subcommand that
operates on one or both repos gets `--scope=code|bot|code,
bot`. No vocabulary forks across commands; `--scope` is the
project-wide convention. Existing per-command flags
(`-R/--repo`, `--repo`) stay as back-compat aliases where
they already exist; new commands inherit `--scope` from day
one.

**Per-command rollout map.**

- `init` — done (0.40.0). `--scope=code|bot|code,bot`.
- `sync` — done (0.41.0-2). `--scope` + `-R` (mutually
  exclusive); `-R` retained for arbitrary multi-repo.
- `push` — 0.41.0-4. State machine becomes scope-aware;
  single-side path skips `commit-claude` /
  `finalize-claude` and bookmarks one repo only.
- `finalize` — 0.41.0-5. `--scope` is canonical; existing
  `--repo` becomes a back-compat alias (overrides scope
  when present, prints a soft deprecation note, slated
  for removal in a future cycle). Does *not* downgrade to
  the "review-only / no-op" outcome the original 0.41.0-4
  sketch hedged toward.
- `clone` — post-0.41.0. Parallel to `init --scope`;
  single-repo clone for `vc-template-x1`-shape remotes.
- `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` — post-0.41.0. Same
  vocabulary as everywhere else, no rename to `--side`.
  Semantic: `--scope=code` validates code's commits
  against bot, `--scope=bot` reverses the direction,
  `--scope=code,bot` does both (the new default).
- `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show` — post-0.41.0. Add
  `--scope` via `CommonArgs` so all four inherit it
  in one cycle. Existing `-R` / `--repo` stays alongside.
- `symlink` — N/A. Bot-only by definition; scope doesn't
  apply.

**Deferred design questions** (caught in todo, not blocking
the rollout):

- `[workspace]` section name in `.vc-config.toml` reads as
  a Cargo workspace to Rust users but isn't one. Rename
  candidates: `[repo-list]` / `[project]` / `[dual-repo]`.
  Breaking change; needs migration.
- Help layout uniformity — clap auto-picks two-column vs
  over-under per command based on widest flag spec.
  `sync -h` is two-column, `init -h` is over-under. Force
  uniform via `next_line_help(true)` at the root.

Edits:

- `Cargo.toml`: bump to `0.41.0-3`.
- `CLAUDE.md`: `### Versioning` cross-references the new
  In Progress dev-ladder convention.
- `notes/chores-06.md`: refine the [71] cycle-step sketch
  to insert this `-3` notes step and renumber push (was
  `-3`) and finalize (was `-4`); add this subsection.
- `notes/todo.md`: three new Todo entries — `clone`
  `--scope`, `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` `--scope`,
  `CommonArgs` (`chid`/`desc`/`list`/`show`) `--scope`.
  In Progress reshaped — explanatory intro paragraph
  shows the dev-ladder format, and the active entry is
  rewritten as the bulleted ladder with
  `(done)` / `(current)` markers.

### 0.41.0-4: capture --scope enum vocabulary

Notes-only step. Mid-implementation rethink — the
original `-4` (push --scope on top of `Vec<Side>`)
exposed a vocabulary gap that the user surfaced
2026-04-26: `code` plays two distinct roles depending on
context (a *role* in a dual workspace; an implicit
*fallback to cwd* in POR). Conflating them muddies what
"obvious to the user" looks like. Splitting them honestly
is a type-model change, not a per-command implementation
detail — so the cycle pivots: capture the refined
vocabulary here, defer all command implementations to
0.42.0, and close 0.41.0 cleanly.

**Settled vocabulary.**

- `--scope` (short form `-s`) accepts:
  - `code` — the app side of a dual-repo workspace.
  - `bot` — the bot side of a dual-repo workspace.
  - `code,bot` / `bot,code` — both (commutative; same
    semantic, same default).
  - `<path>` — single-repo mode; ignore `.vc-config.toml`,
    operate on the given path. Path values must be
    prefixed (`./`, `/`, `~/`) to disambiguate from the
    keyword set. Tilde expansion mirrors init's existing
    behavior on `--repo-local` / `--repo-remote`.
- No discoverable `single` keyword. `--scope=.` is the
  cwd form; `--scope=/abs/path` or `--scope=./rel` is the
  explicit form. Bare names that aren't keywords error
  with a "did you mean `./foo`?" hint.

**Internal type model.**

```rust
pub enum Scope {
    Roles(Vec<Side>), // code | bot | code,bot
    Single(PathBuf),  // explicit path, single-repo mode
}
```

Replaces today's `Scope(pub Vec<Side>)`. The current
`has_code` / `has_bot` / `is_code_only` etc. helpers all
shift to operate on `Roles` only — `Single(_)` is a
distinct mode, not a one-element role list.

**Default-scope rules (revised).**

| Workspace state | `.vc-config.toml` shape | Default scope |
|---|---|---|
| Dual workspace | `path` + non-empty `other-repo` | `Roles([Code, Bot])` |
| Single-repo workspace | `path`, no `other-repo` | `Roles([Code])` |
| POR | (no file) | `Single(cwd)` |

The single-repo-workspace concept stays — it's a real,
distinct state with a non-trivial default (`Roles([Code])`
not `Single(...)`). `--scope=bot` errors there with
"no other-repo configured."

**Per-command applicability matrix.**

| Command | `Roles` accepted | `Single(_)` accepted |
|---|---|---|
| `init` | yes | no — init creates workspaces, not POR repos |
| `sync` | yes | yes |
| `push` | yes | yes |
| `finalize` | yes (currently has `--repo`; migrates to `--scope`) | yes |
| `clone` | yes | yes (single-repo clone target) |
| `validate-desc` | yes | **error** — validate compares two repos by definition; `Single` is meaningless |
| `fix-desc` | yes | **error** (same reason as validate-desc) |
| `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show` | yes | yes |
| `symlink` | n/a | n/a |

`Single` is *always* available as the explicit
"ignore-the-config" escape hatch on commands where it
applies, even inside a dual workspace — the user
explicitly bypasses the dispatcher. On commands where it
can't apply (validate-desc / fix-desc), errors with a
specific message rather than silently doing the wrong
thing.

**Flag-surface changes.**

- Drop `-R` / `--repo` everywhere. Currently sync has it
  (mutually exclusive with `--scope`); finalize has its
  own `--repo` (single repo). Both fold into the new
  `--scope=<path>` form.
- `--scope` gets short form `-s` for ergonomics.
- The path form covers what `-R` / `--repo` did before.
  Multi-repo (sync's old `-R . -R .claude` style) is
  deliberately not preserved — that capability had no
  real users beyond manually emulating today's default.

**Rationale for the cycle pivot.**

- The 0.41.0-3 capture committed to "consistency
  everywhere", which means push, finalize, and the
  remaining commands all need to agree on the type
  model. Implementing push on the soon-to-be-changed
  type would be churn.
- The enum refactor touches scope.rs, common.rs,
  init, sync, plus push and finalize at the same time.
  That's a 0.42.0-sized cycle, not a sub-step inside
  0.41.0.
- Closing 0.41.0 here produces a clean checkpoint: the
  scope foundation (0.40.0 init) and one consumer
  (0.41.0-2 sync) are shipped, the direction is captured
  in 0.41.0-3 and 0.41.0-4. 0.42.0 opens with the type
  refactor as its `-0` plan.

Edits:

- `Cargo.toml`: bump to `0.41.0-4` (notes-only step).
- `src/push.rs`: revert the partial scope work that was
  in flight (`--scope` flag on `PushArgs`, scope-related
  imports). Push stays unchanged on main; its
  scope wiring lands in 0.42.0 against the new type.
- `notes/chores-06.md`: cycle-step sketch updated (0.41.0-3
  marked shipped; 0.41.0-4 redefined as the enum
  capture; 0.41.0-5 dropped from the cycle); this
  subsection added; legacy default-scope rules marked
  superseded.
- `notes/todo.md`: dev-ladder updated (0.41.0-3 → done;
  0.41.0-4 current; 0.41.0-5 removed; 0.41.0 close-out
  noted).

### 0.41.0: cycle close-out

lastPrompt: hmm, CommonArgs is really a bundle and it should be in options_flags

content.text:
Reading the prior design history, "the full sweep" is the long-planned **"CommonArgs sweep"** — it's well-specified in `chores-06.md` ("Generalize --scope across commands", the 0.41.0-3/-4 captures) and `chores-07.md` ("--scope enum refactor (0.42.0)"). Three things bundled:

1. **Migration A** — `XxxParams` + `fn x(&Context, &XxxParams)` ports for chid/desc/list/show.
2. **Migration B** — decompose `CommonArgs`'s fields into `options_flags/` leaves; the bundle relocates to `src/options_flags/`.
3. **`--scope` rollout** — replace `-R`/`--repo` with `-s`/`--scope` (`code|bot|code,bot|<path>`), wiring up `scope.rs`'s `parse_scope` + `Scope::Single` (currently `#[allow(dead_code)]` — they exist *waiting for exactly this*) plus `common.rs`'s `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos` (already there).

Things that follow from the captured design (so you know what you're approving):

- **`-R`/`--repo` goes away.** The 0.42.0 capture deliberately drops it everywhere — the `--scope=<path>` form covers single-repo; arbitrary multi-repo (`-R . -R .claude`) is *not* preserved ("had no real users beyond emulating today's default"). So `chid -R .claude` becomes `chid -s ./.claude`, and the default behavior is `default_scope()` (dual workspace → both repos).
- **All four `From` become `TryFrom`** — scope→repo resolution is fallible (`--scope=bot` in a POR errors).
- **`for_each_repo` simplifies** — takes an already-resolved `Vec<PathBuf>`; the comma-expansion + `["."]` default move to the boundary.
- **`init`'s `--scope` is left alone** — it uses the separate `ScopeKind` (`code,bot|por`); reconciling the two `--scope` vocabularies is the separate "por/dual parity" todo, not this cycle.

Proposed ladder (keeps the chid work already staged):

- `0.49.0-1` chid + `CommonParams` — **staged**; I'll just rewrite its ARCHITECTURE.md/todo framing to the expanded plan (and add the missing `Cargo.toml` bump). Still ports against `-R` for now.
- `0.49.0-2` desc · `0.49.0-3` list · `0.49.0-4` show — Migration A ports.
- `0.49.0-5` decompose `CommonArgs` → `options_flags/` leaves + relocate to `src/options_flags/common_bundle.rs`. Pure refactor.
- `0.49.0-6` `-R`/`--repo` → `-s`/`--scope`: wire `parse_scope`/`default_scope`/`scope_to_repos`, flip the four `From`→`TryFrom`, slim `for_each_repo`, update tests + `README.md` + `ARCHITECTURE.md`, mark the "CommonArgs sweep" todo done + Migration A 12/12. (May split into `-6.N` sub-steps.)
- `0.49.0` close-out.

Two things to confirm:

1. **Order** — A-first (above; keeps the staged chid work, costs 4 trivial `From`→`TryFrom` edits in `-6`), or scope+B-first (cleaner dependency order, but means discarding the staged `0.49.0-1` and restarting)? I lean A-first.
2. **`0.49.0-0`** — it's pushed with the title "finish Migration A", now too narrow. Leave it and record the expanded scope in `0.49.0-1`'s chores section (history shows the plan evolved), or amend it via `--ignore-immutable`+force-push? I lean leave-it.

OK to proceed on A-first + leave-`0.49.0-0`?

content: Goal: expand the 0.49.0 cycle to the full "CommonArgs sweep" for chid/desc/list/show (Migration A + B + `--scope`). The chid port plus CommonParams is staged but unpushed. Next: get your OK on the proposed A-first ladder and leaving `0.49.0-0` as-is, then proceed. (disable recaps in /config)

## refactor: chid → Context + ChidParams (0.49.0-1)

Step 1 of the cycle, and the point where the cycle is
re-scoped (see below). `chid` is the smallest of the four:
`#[command(flatten)]` of `CommonArgs`, body is `for_each_repo`
+ `collect_ids` + print. The port also introduces the shared
`CommonParams` the other three will reuse.

- `common::CommonParams` (flat, clap-free): the resolved
  `DotSpec` + `Header` + raw `repos: Vec<PathBuf>`, with
  `impl From<&CommonArgs>` running `resolve_spec` (default rev
  `@`) and `resolve_header` at the binary edge — the `..`
  parsing and `-l`/`-L` resolution leave the op bodies.
- `ChidParams { common: CommonParams }` + `impl From<&ChidArgs>`;
  `chid(args)` → `chid(_ctx: &Context, params: &ChidParams)`,
  `ctx` the uniform-signature placeholder (as in symlink /
  validate-desc / fix-desc). `chid.rs` also gains the `//!`
  module docstring it was missing.
- `main.rs` `Chid` arm builds `Context::load(cli.log)` +
  `ChidParams::from`; `suppress_banner` still reads
  `a.common.no_label` off the args (clap edge).

### Cycle re-scope (0.49.0-1)

The 0.49.0-0 plan opened a "finish Migration A" cycle for the
four `CommonArgs`-flattening subcommands. During 0.49.0-1
review the cycle was expanded to the full **"CommonArgs sweep"**
long planned in `chores-06` / `chores-07` — Migration A is
joined by Migration B and the `--scope` rollout for these four.
(The 0.49.0-0 commit and its chores section keep the original
narrow framing; this subsection is the record of the change of
plan.)

- **Migration B** — decompose `CommonArgs`'s fields into
  `options_flags/` leaves (`revision`, the `-n` commit limit,
  the `-l`/`-L` label pair, the two positionals) and relocate
  the bundle to `src/options_flags/common_bundle.rs`. Pure
  refactor; the `From<&CommonArgs> for CommonParams` boundary
  is the only consumer that changes shape (reads `.value`).
- **`--scope`** — replace the repeatable `-R`/`--repo` with
  `-s`/`--scope` (`code|bot|code,bot|<path>`), wiring up
  `scope.rs`'s `parse_scope` + `Scope::Single` (both currently
  `#[allow(dead_code)]` — built in 0.42.0 for exactly this) and
  `common.rs`'s `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`. Per the
  0.42.0 capture (`chores-06` 0.41.0-4): `-R` is dropped, not
  aliased; arbitrary multi-repo (`-R . -R .claude`) is not
  preserved — `--scope=<path>` covers single-repo, the default
  is `default_scope()`. `for_each_repo` then takes an
  already-resolved `Vec<PathBuf>` (comma-expansion + `["."]`
  default move to the boundary), and all four `From`s become
  `TryFrom` (scope→repo resolution is fallible). `init`'s
  `--scope` (the separate `ScopeKind` `code,bot|por`) is left
  alone — reconciling the two vocabularies is the "por/dual
  parity" todo.

Revised ladder (supersedes the 0.49.0-0 one):

- 0.49.0-1 chid Migration A + `CommonParams` + this re-scope
- 0.49.0-2 desc Migration A
- 0.49.0-3 list Migration A
- 0.49.0-4 show Migration A (`TryFrom`, `FileLimit` parse)
- 0.49.0-5 Migration B — `CommonArgs` → `options_flags/`
  leaf-bundle + relocate
- 0.49.0-6 `-R`/`--repo` → `-s`/`--scope` (may split into
  `-6.N` sub-steps)
- 0.49.0 close-out — drop suffix, todo→Done (Migration A
  12/12 + CommonArgs sweep), README + ARCHITECTURE.md

# References

185	## Migration A — args → Context + Params
186	
187	Port each subcommand's `pub fn x(args: &XxxArgs)` to
188	`pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams)`, adding an
189	`XxxParams` flat struct + a `From` (or `TryFrom`, if the
190	conversion is fallible) at the binary edge. `init` (0.44.0) is
191	the worked example; `finalize` (0.46.0) added the `TryFrom` /
192	`Context.log` variant; the `0.48.0` cycle swept the standalone
193	subcommands; the `0.49.0` cycle finishes the four
194	`CommonArgs`-flattening ones (`chid` -1, `desc` -2, `list` -3,
195	`show` -4) — these embed a shared clap-free `CommonParams`. Their
196	Migration B (folding `CommonArgs` into the `options_flags/` leaf
197	model) stays the separate "CommonArgs sweep" todo. The live
198	checklist is the "Subcommand layer / CLI decoupling" item in
199	[`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md).
200	
201	| Subcommand | Status |
202	| --- | --- |
203	| `init` | done (0.44.0) — `init(&Context, &InitParams)`; `From<&InitArgs>` |
204	| `finalize` | done (0.46.0) — `finalize(&Context, &FinalizeParams)`; `TryFrom<&FinalizeArgs>` (fallible boundary); `--log` moved onto `Context` |
205	| `symlink` | done (0.48.0-1) — `symlink(&Context, &SymlinkParams)`; `From<&SymlinkArgs>` |
206	| `clone` | done (0.48.0-2) — `clone_repo(&Context, &CloneParams)`; `From<&CloneArgs>` |
207	| `sync` | done (0.48.0-3) — `sync(&Context, &SyncParams)`; `From<&SyncArgs>` |
208	| `validate-desc` | done (0.48.0-4) — `validate_desc(&Context, &ValidateDescParams)`; `From<&ValidateDescArgs>` |
209	| `fix-desc` | done (0.48.0-5) — `fix_desc(&Context, &FixDescParams)`; `From<&FixDescArgs>` |
210	| `push` | done (0.48.0-6) — `push(&Context, &PushParams)`; `From<&PushArgs>` (collapses the two bookmark spellings) |
211	| `chid` | done (0.49.0-1) — `chid(&Context, &ChidParams)`; `From<&ChidArgs>` (wraps `CommonParams`) |
212	| `desc` | Migration A pending (0.49.0-2) — chid/desc/list/show port; Migration B on the CommonArgs sweep |
213	| `list` | Migration A pending (0.49.0-3) — chid/desc/list/show port; Migration B on the CommonArgs sweep |
214	| `show` | Migration A pending (0.49.0-4) — chid/desc/list/show port (`TryFrom`, `FileLimit` parse); Migration B on the CommonArgs sweep |
215	
216	Out of scope for these ports (deferred until a real consumer
217	surfaces): typed errors, returned outcomes vs `println!`, the
218	`ProgressSink`, and `Context` fields beyond `UserConfig` + the
219	`--log` path (`finalize` surfaced `log`).

file.content:
## Migration A — args → Context + Params

Port each subcommand's `pub fn x(args: &XxxArgs)` to
`pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams)`, adding an
`XxxParams` flat struct + a `From` (or `TryFrom`, if the
conversion is fallible) at the binary edge. `init` (0.44.0) is
the worked example; `finalize` (0.46.0) added the `TryFrom` /
`Context.log` variant; the `0.48.0` cycle swept the standalone
subcommands; the `0.49.0` cycle finishes the four
`CommonArgs`-flattening ones (`chid` -1, `desc` -2, `list` -3,
`show` -4) — these embed a shared clap-free `CommonParams`. Their
Migration B (folding `CommonArgs` into the `options_flags/` leaf
model) stays the separate "CommonArgs sweep" todo. The live
checklist is the "Subcommand layer / CLI decoupling" item in
[`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md).

| Subcommand | Status |
| --- | --- |
| `init` | done (0.44.0) — `init(&Context, &InitParams)`; `From<&InitArgs>` |
| `finalize` | done (0.46.0) — `finalize(&Context, &FinalizeParams)`; `TryFrom<&FinalizeArgs>` (fallible boundary); `--log` moved onto `Context` |
| `symlink` | done (0.48.0-1) — `symlink(&Context, &SymlinkParams)`; `From<&SymlinkArgs>` |
| `clone` | done (0.48.0-2) — `clone_repo(&Context, &CloneParams)`; `From<&CloneArgs>` |
| `sync` | done (0.48.0-3) — `sync(&Context, &SyncParams)`; `From<&SyncArgs>` |
| `validate-desc` | done (0.48.0-4) — `validate_desc(&Context, &ValidateDescParams)`; `From<&ValidateDescArgs>` |
| `fix-desc` | done (0.48.0-5) — `fix_desc(&Context, &FixDescParams)`; `From<&FixDescArgs>` |
| `push` | done (0.48.0-6) — `push(&Context, &PushParams)`; `From<&PushArgs>` (collapses the two bookmark spellings) |
| `chid` | done (0.49.0-1) — `chid(&Context, &ChidParams)`; `From<&ChidArgs>` (wraps `CommonParams`) |
| `desc` | Migration A pending (0.49.0-2) — chid/desc/list/show port; Migration B on the CommonArgs sweep |
| `list` | Migration A pending (0.49.0-3) — chid/desc/list/show port; Migration B on the CommonArgs sweep |
| `show` | Migration A pending (0.49.0-4) — chid/desc/list/show port (`TryFrom`, `FileLimit` parse); Migration B on the CommonArgs sweep |

Out of scope for these ports (deferred until a real consumer
surfaces): typed errors, returned outcomes vs `println!`, the
`ProgressSink`, and `Context` fields beyond `UserConfig` + the
`--log` path (`finalize` surfaced `log`).

## Module map

CLI layer:

- `src/main.rs` — the clap edge:
  - `pub struct Cli` — clap `Parser`.
  - `pub enum Commands` — clap `Subcommand`; matched in the
    dispatch arms.
  - `cli_with_banner()` — walks the command tree adding the
    `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner as `before_help` on every subcommand.
  - `CompleteEnv` wiring for dynamic completion.
  - `bm_track` — a permanent `main`-bookmark tracking
    diagnostic printed on entry/exit of every command.

- `src/options_flags/` — options/flags shared across
  subcommands, so a flag is defined once (parser, help,
  completer); Migration B tracks what's been moved here so
  far. See
  [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md).
  - reusable **leaves**: `account`, `config`, `dry_run`,
    `private`, `push_retry`, `repo`, `scope`, `squash`,
    `use_template`.
  - reusable **bundles**: `provision_bundle`.
- `src/common.rs` — `CommonArgs` (the shared positional-rev /
  `-R` repo-list / `-n` / `--limit` / `-L` flag set flattened
  by `chid`, `desc`, `list`, `show`) plus the repo-iteration
  and revision-resolution helpers they share.

Subcommand layer scaffolding:

- `src/context.rs` — `Context`: the shared platform handle
  every subcommand runs against. Today it carries the loaded
  `UserConfig` and the `--log` path; built once at startup
  via `Context::load(log)`.
- `src/config.rs` — `UserConfig` (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`).
- `src/scope.rs` — `Scope` enum (`Roles(Vec<Side>)` for the
  dual-repo `code`/`bot` roles, `Single(PathBuf)` for
  single-repo mode) and `parse_scope`.

Subcommand modules — each holds an `XxxArgs` `#[derive(Args)]`
struct and a `pub fn x(...)` entrypoint:

| Module | Subcommand | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `chid.rs` | `chid` | migrated (0.49.0-1) — `chid(&Context, &ChidParams)`, `From<&ChidArgs>`; flattens `CommonArgs` (→ `CommonParams`) |
| `desc.rs` (+ `desc_helpers.rs`) | `desc` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `list.rs` | `list` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `show.rs` | `show` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `validate_desc.rs` | `validate-desc` | migrated (0.48.0-4) — `validate_desc(&Context, &ValidateDescParams)`, `From<&ValidateDescArgs>` |
| `fix_desc.rs` | `fix-desc` | migrated (0.48.0-5) — `fix_desc(&Context, &FixDescParams)`, `From<&FixDescArgs>` |
| `clone.rs` | `clone` | migrated (0.48.0-2) — `clone_repo(&Context, &CloneParams)`, `From<&CloneArgs>` |
| `init.rs` (+ `init/params.rs`) | `init` | subcommand-layer worked example (0.44.0) |
| `symlink.rs` | `symlink` | migrated (0.48.0-1) — `symlink(&Context, &SymlinkParams)`, `From<&SymlinkArgs>` |
| `sync.rs` | `sync` | migrated (0.48.0-3) — `sync(&Context, &SyncParams)`, `From<&SyncArgs>` |
| `finalize.rs` | `finalize` | migrated (0.46.0) — `finalize(&Context, &FinalizeParams)`, `TryFrom<&FinalizeArgs>` |

**Code (the chid Migration A port + shared `CommonParams`):**

- **`src/chid.rs`** — full rewrite: added the missing `//!` docstring; `ChidParams { common: CommonParams }` + `impl From<&ChidArgs>`; `chid(args)` → `chid(_ctx: &Context, params: &ChidParams)`; all existing parse tests kept + a `params_from_args` boundary-conversion test. (rustfmt reflowed the `collect_ids` call multi-line.)
- **`src/main.rs`** — `Chid` dispatch arm now builds `Context::load(cli.log)` + `ChidParams::from`.

**Cycle re-scope to the full "CommonArgs sweep" (A + B + `--scope`):**
- **`Cargo.toml` + `Cargo.lock`** — `0.49.0-0` → `0.49.0-1` (the version bump I'd missed at step start).
- **`notes/todo.md`** — In Progress ladder rewritten: `0.49.0-0` done, `0.49.0-1` current (chid + re-scope), then `-2` desc / `-3` list / `-4` show (Migration A), `-5` Migration B (decompose `CommonArgs` → `options_flags/` leaf-bundle + relocate), `-6` `-R`/`--repo` → `-s`/`--scope` (may split into `-6.N`), close-out. Links to the chores-09 re-scope subsection + the prior `--scope` design in chores-06/-07.
- **`notes/chores-09.md`** — new `## refactor: chid → Context + ChidParams (0.49.0-1)` section (no `Commits:` line — it's the newest; backfilled next cycle start) with a `### Cycle re-scope (0.49.0-1)` subsection recording the expansion (what Migration B + `--scope` entail; that `-R` is dropped not aliased per the 0.42.0 capture; that `init`'s `--scope` is left alone) and the revised ladder. The `0.49.0-0` commit + its chores section keep the original narrow framing — this subsection is the record of the plan change.
- **`ARCHITECTURE.md`** — Migration A intro reframed (0.49.0 = the CommonArgs sweep, A then B then `--scope`); `chid` row → done (0.49.0-1, B/`--scope` in -5/-6); desc/list/show rows → "the 0.49.0 CommonArgs sweep"; `common.rs` module-map entry now mentions `CommonParams` + the planned relocation; Migration B section's chid/desc/list/show bullet now describes the in-flight -5/-6 work.

Please review. On approval I'll prep the commit title/body for a second approval before pushing.

message.content: Hmm, why not decompose CommonArgs first (i.e. Migration B first)?

content.text:
Good call — B-first *is* cleaner, for a concrete reason: Migration A's shape (the `From` vs `TryFrom` boundary, what `CommonParams` carries) depends on what `CommonArgs` ends up looking like. If A goes first against today's fat `CommonArgs` (with `-R`), then `-5` decomposes it (the `From<&CommonArgs> for CommonParams` internals change to read `.value`) and `-6` swaps `-R`→`--scope` (the four `From`s flip to `TryFrom`, and `CommonParams.repos` changes source) — so every A-port gets re-touched twice. B-and-scope first means the A-ports are written *once*, against the final shape. The only thing A-first buys is keeping the chid code I already staged — but that's ~30 lines, cheap to re-apply later.

Revised ladder (B-first):

- **`0.49.0-1`** — Migration B: decompose `CommonArgs`'s non-repo fields into `options_flags/` leaves (`revision`, the `-n` commit limit, the `-l`/`-L` label pair, the two positionals), relocate the bundle to `src/options_flags/common_bundle.rs` (still carrying `repos: Vec<PathBuf>` for `-R`). Pure refactor — all four subcommands keep working. Carries the cycle re-scope docs too. (May split into `-1.N`.)
- **`0.49.0-2`** — `-R`/`--repo` → `-s`/`--scope`: swap the bundle's `repos` for a new `scope` leaf; wire `scope.rs`'s `parse_scope` + `Scope::Single` and `common.rs`'s `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`; slim `for_each_repo` to take resolved repos; update `README.md`. Behavior change. (May split into `-2.N`.)
- **`0.49.0-3`** — chid Migration A + introduce `CommonParams` (carrying the resolved `repos`); `TryFrom<&ChidArgs>`.
- **`0.49.0-4`** desc · **`0.49.0-5`** list · **`0.49.0-6`** show — Migration A (`TryFrom`).
- **`0.49.0`** — close-out.

What this means for what's staged: I revert the chid port code (chid.rs / common.rs `CommonParams` / main.rs `Chid` arm / the chid module-map row) from the working copy and rework `0.49.0-1` into the Migration B step. The re-scope docs (todo ladder, the chores `### Cycle re-scope` subsection, the ARCHITECTURE.md reframe) stay, just with the new ladder. `0.49.0-0` stays as-is on the remote.

OK to reorder to B-first? (And — leave `0.49.0-1` numbering as is, i.e. the Migration B step becomes `0.49.0-1`?)

lastPrompt: Hmm, why not decompose CommonArgs first (i.e. Migration B first)?

35	/// Banner string emitted as the first line of normal command runs
36	/// and shown at the top of subcommand `--help` output. Built from
37	/// `Cargo.toml`'s name + version at compile time so it stays in
38	/// sync with the bumped version.
39	const BANNER: &str = concat!(env!("CARGO_PKG_NAME"), " ", env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION"));
40	
41	/// Top-level about line — name, version, and the project tagline
42	/// on a single line. Used as the top-level `about` so `vc-x1 -h`
43	/// reads as one banner-plus-tagline header instead of two stacked
44	/// lines.
45	const TOP_ABOUT: &str = concat!(
46	    env!("CARGO_PKG_NAME"),
47	    " ",
48	    env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION"),
49	    " - jj workspace tooling"
50	);
51	
52	/// Build the clap command tree with `BANNER` set as `before_help`
53	/// on every subcommand (transitively). Top-level skips `before_help`
54	/// because its own `about` already carries the name+version+tagline.
55	/// Walks via `mut_subcommand` so individual subcommand
56	/// `#[command(long_about = ...)]` blocks don't have to repeat the
57	/// banner text.
58	fn cli_with_banner() -> clap::Command {
59	    fn add_to_subs(mut cmd: clap::Command) -> clap::Command {
60	        let names: Vec<String> = cmd
61	            .get_subcommands()
62	            .map(|c| c.get_name().to_string())
63	            .collect();
64	        for name in names {
65	            cmd = cmd.mut_subcommand(name, add_with_banner);
66	        }
67	        cmd
68	    }
69	    fn add_with_banner(mut cmd: clap::Command) -> clap::Command {
70	        cmd = cmd.before_help(BANNER);
71	        let names: Vec<String> = cmd
72	            .get_subcommands()
73	            .map(|c| c.get_name().to_string())
74	            .collect();
75	        for name in names {
76	            cmd = cmd.mut_subcommand(name, add_with_banner);
77	        }
78	        cmd
79	    }
80	    add_to_subs(Cli::command())
81	}
82	
83	#[derive(Parser, Debug)]
84	#[command(
85	    version,
86	    propagate_version = true,
87	    about = TOP_ABOUT,
88	    max_term_width = 80
89	)]
90	pub struct Cli {
91	    /// Verbose output: -v debug, -vv trace
92	    #[arg(short, long, global = true, action = clap::ArgAction::Count)]
93	    pub verbose: u8,
94	
95	    /// Log file path (captures all levels)
96	    #[arg(long, global = true)]
97	    pub log: Option<std::path::PathBuf>,
98	
99	    #[command(subcommand)]

101	}

124	          miss — no ochid trailer; shows match from other repo if found"
125	    )]
126	    ValidateDesc(validate_desc::ValidateDescArgs),
127	
128	    /// Fix commit descriptions against the other repo (dry-run by default)
129	    #[command(long_about = "Fix commit descriptions against the other repo.\n\n\
130	        Default is dry-run; use --no-dry-run to write changes.\n\n\
131	        Output columns: STATUS  CHANGEID  TITLE  [DETAILS]\n\n\
132	        Status labels:\n  \
133	          ok    — ochid trailer is valid (no change)\n  \
134	          fix   — ochid has issues, shows proposed fix (dry-run)\n  \
135	          fixed — ochid was rewritten (--no-dry-run)\n  \
136	          add   — missing ochid, match found, shows proposed addition (dry-run)\n  \
137	          added — missing ochid was added (--no-dry-run)\n  \
138	          skip  — skipped (no ochid, no match, or max-fixes reached)\n  \
139	          err   — ID not found and no --fallback provided")]
140	    FixDesc(fix_desc::FixDescArgs),
141	
142	    /// Clone a dual-repo project
143	    Clone(clone::CloneArgs),
144	
145	    /// Create a new dual-repo project
146	    Init(init::InitArgs),
147	
148	    /// Create Claude Code project symlink
149	    Symlink(symlink::SymlinkArgs),
150	
151	    /// Fetch and sync a set of repos to their remotes
152	    #[command(long_about = "Fetch and sync a set of repos to their remotes.\n\n\
153	        Repo set is resolved (in order):\n  \
154	          - `-R` / `--repo`     exact list (back-compat / arbitrary multi-repo)\n  \
155	          - `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` dual-repo roles via `.vc-config.toml`\n  \
156	          - neither             default: `code,bot` when dual, else `code`\n\n\
157	        Default is --check (verify only) — fatal if any repo needs\n\
158	        action. Re-run with --no-check to apply (rebase / fast-forward).\n\
159	        Per repo:\n  \
160	          - up-to-date        nothing to do\n  \
161	          - behind            fast-forward bookmark to remote\n  \
162	          - ahead             nothing to sync (local has unpushed work)\n  \
163	          - diverged          rebase local onto remote; fail on conflicts\n  \
164	          - no remote         bookmark has no @<remote> counterpart; skip\n\n\
165	        Scripts and automation should pass --check or --no-check\n\
166	        explicitly — defaults can shift, explicit flags lock in the\n\
167	        contract. Interactive use can rely on the default.\n\n\
168	        After the bookmark action, `@` is rebased onto the (possibly\n\
169	        advanced) bookmark if it isn't already a descendant, so trailing\n\
170	        working-copy writes (e.g. `.claude`'s `/exit` tail) don't end up\n\
171	        orphaned on a stale branch.\n\n\
172	        On any failure, every repo is reverted to its starting state via\n\
173	        `jj op restore`. Working-copy files are preserved across the\n\
174	        revert — the operation log rewinds but disk content stays.\n\n\
175	        Output shape:\n  \
176	          - all-up-to-date: one-line summary (`sync: N repos, all bookmarks up-to-date`)\n  \
177	          - action needed (--check):  per-repo fetch + state + fatal error\n  \
178	          - action needed (--no-check): per-repo fetch + state + actions\n  \
179	          - --quiet:        no output; exit code signals success")]
180	    Sync(sync::SyncArgs),
181	
182	    /// Squash, set bookmark, and/or push a jj repo
183	    #[command(long_about = "Squash, set bookmark, and/or push a jj repo.\n\n\

185	        --detach exits immediately, --delay waits for trailing writes,\n\
186	        --squash folds them in, --bookmark + --push sends it upstream.\n\
187	        Every flag is opt-in. See README.md for details.")]
